contextSummary: WordPress's next-generation editor. The new @wordpress/ui package builds its entire Popover family on Base UI, one file per part, with slot-overridable Portal/Positioner (for the iframed editor canvas), an optional Backdrop, dev-time validation that a Title is present, and a hook that deprioritizes Close buttons when resolving initial focus.
rankRationale: The richest Base UI popover composition found anywhere: WordPress's new @wordpress/ui package wraps every part (incl. Backdrop) one-file-per-part, adds slot-overridable Portal/Positioner for the iframed editor canvas, dev-time validation that a Title is present ('required for accessibility, can be visually hidden'), a Close-required-when-modal rule, and a useDeprioritizedInitialFocus hook that skips close buttons when resolving initial focus. Feeds the anatomy, accessibility-contract, and prop-guidance (initialFocus, modal, Backdrop) doc sections; a recomposed story could demo backdrop + focus-deprioritization. GPL/dual licensing = link-only, but as evidence it is unmatched.

contextSummary: Cross-platform Tauri music player (museeks.io). The header's play-queue popover anchors to a wrapper div via anchor={queueAnchorRef} (decoupled from the Trigger), composes the Trigger over a custom ButtonIcon via render, and styles with StyleX.
rankRationale: Real app-level usage (not a wrapper): the play-queue popover in a Tauri music player's title bar. Shows anchor={ref} decoupled from the Trigger (popup aligns to a wrapper div, not the icon button), Trigger composed over a custom ButtonIcon via the render prop, StyleX styling, and align='end'/alignOffset tuning. MIT + live site (museeks.io) + 2.1k stars. Story recomposition: 'queue popover' — icon trigger, custom anchor, end-aligned panel; feeds the anchor prop-guidance and render-prop sections.

contextSummary: AI markdown editor (flashtype.com). The formatting toolbar's link editor is a Popover whose Trigger is composed into Toolbar.Button via render (nativeButton={false}), openOnHover disabled, and initialFocus pointed at the URL input — a form-in-popover inside a roving-tabindex toolbar.
rankRationale: Form-in-popover inside a roving-tabindex Toolbar: the markdown editor's link editor composes Toolbar.Button render={<Popover.Trigger/>} with nativeButton={false}, disables openOnHover, and sets initialFocus={linkInputRef} so the URL input focuses on open. The best found example of cross-component composition (popover x toolbar) and deliberate focus routing. MIT, live (flashtype.com). Story: 'edit link' toolbar popover with play-function typing a URL; feeds composition and initialFocus guidance.

contextSummary: Dify's internal design system (packages/dify-ui) wraps Popover behind a single `placement` prop parsed into side/align, re-exports createHandle, and animates via data-starting/ending-style with motion-reduce support. Serves the flagship LLM app platform's UI.
rankRationale: Highest-star adopter (147.9k): Dify's dify-ui design system wraps popover behind a single `placement` prop parsed to side/align, re-exports createHandle for detached triggering, and demonstrates the canonical data-starting/ending-style scale/opacity transition with motion-reduce fallback. Custom license = link-only, but it is the flagship 'production platform standardizes on Base UI popover' citation; feeds the styling/animation contract section.

contextSummary: Personal AI assistant by egoist. @-mention file autocomplete: a triggerless controlled Popover anchored to the chat textarea (anchor={textareaRef}, side='top'), with initialFocus={false}/finalFocus={false} so typing never leaves the textarea; highlight/keyboard nav handled externally.
rankRationale: Triggerless, externally-controlled popover: @-mention file autocomplete anchored to a textarea (Positioner anchor={textareaRef}, side='top'), with initialFocus={false} and finalFocus={false} so keyboard focus never leaves the textarea while arrowing through suggestions. The cleanest evidence for the detached-trigger + focus-opt-out pattern the docs barely cover. Apache-2.0. Story: mention-autocomplete popover with play-function typing '@'.

contextSummary: Unofficial Tailwind implementation of KRDS (Korea government design system). CoachMark renders sequential onboarding steps as an always-open Popover with a hidden zero-size Trigger whose getBoundingClientRect is overridden to a selector-resolved target; ContextualHelp is a second popover-based pattern.
rankRationale: A coach-mark/onboarding-tour archetype from a government design system (Korean KRDS, Tailwind port): one always-open controlled Popover per step, hidden zero-size Trigger whose getBoundingClientRect is overridden to a selector-resolved target, step counter with aria-live, Escape-to-skip. Also instructive as a workaround: Base UI's own anchor prop could replace the rect override — exactly the kind of guidance a 'use the anchor prop when...' doc section should give. Feeds when-to-use (tours/onboarding) and anchor guidance.

contextSummary: GitHub-contributions poster generator (green-wall.leoku.dev). Ships a coss-lineage wrapper using the new Viewport part, createHandle re-export, and a tooltipStyle variant; popovers drive the appearance settings UI.
rankRationale: Production app (GitHub-contributions poster generator, live) shipping the modern coss-lineage wrapper: uses the new Viewport part for size-transition content swaps, re-exports createHandle, and offers a tooltipStyle variant — the newest 1.x popover APIs seen in an MIT app rather than a registry. Story: settings popover with Viewport-animated content; feeds Viewport/createHandle prop guidance.

contextSummary: LyteNyte Grid (commercial React data grid) docs demo: grid 'popover frames' render an always-open Popover per activated cell with anchor={params.target} (the clicked cell element), closed through the grid API — Base UI popover as the grid's cell-attached UI layer.
rankRationale: Popover as a data-grid's cell-attached UI layer: LyteNyte Grid 'popover frames' render an always-open Popover keyed per cell with anchor={params.target} (the clicked cell DOM node) and close through the grid's imperative API. Best example of anchoring to arbitrary, dynamically-chosen elements and of integrating popover lifecycle with an external system. Docs-demo of a commercial grid (NOASSERTION = link-only); feeds anchor + controlled-open guidance.

contextSummary: Open agentic development environment. The prompt InputBar opens an MCP-server selector popover (side='top' align='end') portalled into a custom container element; TaskCreatePage hosts similar option popovers.
rankRationale: Agentic IDE web client: the prompt InputBar's MCP-server selector popover portals into a custom container (Portal container={portalContainer}) and opens side='top' align='end' above the composer — practical evidence for the container prop (stacking/overflow control in complex layouts) and for settings-popover UX in AI tools. No license = link-only.

contextSummary: Russian-language production site (mtiyt.ru). DatePicker hosts react-day-picker inside a controlled Popover and commits the picked date/range on close (onOpenChange(false) → onPick), plus an explicit apply button.
rankRationale: Date-picker-host archetype: react-day-picker rendered inside a controlled Popover; the picked date/range is committed when the popover closes (onOpenChange(false) → onPick) plus an explicit apply button. Live production site. Clean evidence for the 'popover hosting a third-party widget' use case and commit-on-close state pattern; feeds when-to-use and controlled/uncontrolled behavior sections.

contextSummary: AI 3D map editor. The toolbar's 'Generate Object' popover is a small AI-prompt form with Popover.Title and Popover.Close, glassmorphism styling, and streaming generation status.
rankRationale: AI-prompt form popover in a 3D map editor's toolbar: uses Title + Close semantics correctly, hosts an async generate flow (streaming status inside the popup) — a compact real 'form in popover with lifecycle' example under MIT with a live demo. Story: generate-object popover with interaction test on the Close button.

contextSummary: Web3 governance tool. A generic Tooltip component is built from Popover.Root openOnHover delay={0} with an Arrow, rendering Markdown titles; trigger clicks are stopPropagation'd. Instructive: rich/interactive hover content on Popover vs the dedicated Tooltip component's non-interactive semantics.
rankRationale: The labeled instructive-misuse pick: a generic Tooltip component built from Popover.Root openOnHover delay={0} + Arrow rendering Markdown. Shows why teams reach for popover (rich, selectable, interactive hover content) and what is lost vs the dedicated Tooltip component (describedby semantics, non-interactive contract) — ideal evidence for the popover-vs-tooltip decision boundary section. MIT, live demo.

contextSummary: The Graph's Hypergraph connect app. SpacesCard conditionally composes: in view mode the card is a Popover.Root with an info Popup (+Arrow) describing space visibility; in selection mode the same markup renders a Checkbox instead.
rankRationale: Conditional composition: the same SpacesCard renders Popover.Root/Trigger in view mode but Fragment/Checkbox.Root in selection mode — evidence that Base UI parts compose polymorphically with sibling components. The info-popover (+Arrow, Title, Description) explains space visibility. MIT; The Graph's connect app. Feeds anatomy/composition guidance.

contextSummary: coss ui — the cal.com team's Base UI registry. Popover wrapper re-exports createHandle, wires the new Viewport part for smooth size transitions, offers a tooltipStyle variant, and passes through portalProps/anchor.
rankRationale: Origin of the dominant wrapper lineage: the cal.com team's coss ui registry popover (createHandle re-export, Viewport part, tooltipStyle variant, portalProps/anchor passthrough) is visibly copied into multiple downstream apps in this corpus (Green-Wall and others match it nearly line-for-line). Ranked despite sameness-risk because it documents how the ecosystem standardizes Base UI popover; AGPL = link-only.

contextSummary: Animate UI's Base primitives distribution: Popover integrated with motion/react — AnimatePresence-driven exit animations, a context bridging controlled open state, and a Portal that forces keepMounted so unmount animations can play.
rankRationale: Motion-library integration: Animate UI's base popover keeps the Portal keepMounted (removing it from the public prop surface), bridges controlled open state through context, and drives exit animations with AnimatePresence + motion.div inside Popup render props. The canonical evidence for 'animating Base UI popover with an external animation library' — a documented handbook concern. Link-only (no license file found).
